items.headline: City seeking input on community development grant funding
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission is reviewing applications for federal Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding. This money supports local housing and community projects, so the grants being considered could affect neighborhood services and affordable housing initiatives.

items.headline: Mixed-use housing development approved at 490 East Middlefield Road
items.summary: The City Council approved a development agreement for a new mixed-use residential project on East Middlefield Road. This project will add housing to Mountain View and is moving forward after a second reading.

items.headline: Mountain View applies for state housing incentive funding
items.summary: The city is seeking state funding through California's Prohousing Incentive Program to support housing development. This could unlock grants to help the city meet its housing goals.

items.headline: City seeking public input on $HUD housing grant spending plan
items.summary: Santa Clara is holding a public hearing on how it will use federal housing funds for 2026-27. This is your chance to weigh in on affordable housing and community development priorities before the plan is finalized.

items.headline: Public meeting on safe parking program coming up
items.summary: Sunnyvale is holding a community meeting to discuss its safe parking initiative. This program typically addresses homelessness by providing secure parking spaces for people living in vehicles, so this meeting is a chance for residents to learn about or weigh in on the program.

items.headline: Station Area Task Force reviewing land use plans and design guidelines
items.summary: A city task force is reviewing the land use plan and design guidelines for the Station Area—likely referring to development around the Caltrain/VTA station. This could shape what kinds of housing and commercial projects get built there.

items.headline: City hosting zero waste sustainability meeting
items.summary: A community meeting on 'Smart Steps Towards Zero Waste' brings together the city council and sustainability commission. This suggests Sunnyvale is working toward waste reduction goals and residents can learn about or comment on these efforts.

items.headline: New housing development planned for 10333 N. Wolfe Road
items.summary: The City Council held a study session on a housing development project at this North Cupertino location. This signals a potential new residential project in the area that could affect traffic, schools, and local character.

items.headline: City approves $5.8 million radio system upgrade for all departments
items.summary: Palo Alto is purchasing new multi-band portable and mobile radios for police, fire, and other city services through an 8.4-year lease agreement. This is a major modernization of emergency and city communications infrastructure.

items.headline: Historic building tax breaks expanded in downtown zone
items.summary: The city is considering changes to allow older, historic buildings in the commercial zone to get bigger floor area bonuses and participate in development rights transfers. This could incentivize preservation and renovation of Palo Alto's historic structures.

items.headline: Town updates rules for special events permits
items.summary: Los Gatos adopted an ordinance amending how special events are regulated in town. This could affect how community events, festivals, or gatherings are permitted and organized.

items.headline: Palo Alto redraws retail preservation map for El Camino Real
items.summary: The city updated which areas along El Camino Real are designated for retail preservation under local rules. This affects what kinds of development and uses are allowed in this commercial corridor.
